 {"id":"ft-2xnop","title":"[MEDIUM] pty CommandBuilder serde proptests skip serialized unix umask state","description":"[MEDIUM] Recent commit c664dc39 (test: extend pty serde proptests) added frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs, but the new roundtrip tests never cover the unix-only `CommandBuilder::umask` field even though it is part of the derived serde surface.

Evidence:
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:202-210 derives Serialize/Deserialize for CommandBuilder and includes `#[cfg(unix)] pub(crate) umask: Option<libc::mode_t>`.
- frankenterm/pty/src/cmdbuilder.rs:423-425 exposes `CommandBuilder::umask(...)` to mutate that serialized field.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:35-52 `build_command(...)` never sets umask.
- frankenterm/pty/tests/proptest_cmdbuilder_serde.rs:57-123 only asserts argv/cwd/env/controlling_tty, so a serde regression that drops or corrupts `umask` would still pass.

Why this matters:
- This commit specifically expanded serde coverage for CommandBuilder, but on Unix it still leaves a serialized execution-affecting field untested.
- `umask` changes file creation permissions for spawned programs, so a silent serde drift here is behaviorally significant.

Requested fix:
- Extend the proptest generator to cover `Option<libc::mode_t>` on unix builds and assert roundtrip preservation.
- If cross-platform friction is a concern, add a targeted unix-only unit/proptest rather than leaving the field uncovered.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:52:51.639844Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.030677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:08:27.029943Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-31kaq","title":"proptest(ingest): pin Gap content == current and Content delta.len() < current.len() invariants","description":"proptest_ingest_extract_delta_fuzz.rs pins MR1-MR5 + MR3b (overlap existence) on extract_delta. Two additional shipping contracts are implicit but unchecked: (MR7) every Gap variant returned must carry content == current (the three return sites at ingest.rs:1675, 1713, 1724 all satisfy this, but nothing prevents a future refactor from breaking it); (MR8) when Content(delta) is returned, delta.len() < current.len() strictly — since overlap_len >= 1 for any match in the memchr path. Add both as proptest properties alongside the existing MRs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:20.655704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872719Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:02:49.872352Z","close_reason":"Landed in 6ff97e37 (test(proptest/ingest): add MR7 Gap-content-equals-current and MR8 Content-strictly-shorter invariants). Concurrent-agent commit landed the exact same MR7/MR8 additions I wrote; my da87c368 ended up as a beads-tracker-only commit (four pre-existing blockers: ft-uigpj, ft-s2u51, ft-zv3u9 filed).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-326s9","title":"Fix bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls test — config shape contradicts production logic","description":"# Pre-existing mTLS test failure

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 359 verification work. The test
`distributed::tests::bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls` at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:3805` fails reproducibly with:

    client bundle: MissingClientCaPath

## Reproduction
```
cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features distributed \
    --lib bundle_acceptor_connector_mtls -- --nocapture
```

## Root cause
The test constructs a `client_cfg` with `auth_mode =
DistributedAuthMode::Mtls` but without `client_ca_path` populated:

```rust
let mut client_cfg = DistributedConfig::default();
client_cfg.enabled = true;
client_cfg.auth_mode = DistributedAuthMode::Mtls;
client_cfg.tls.enabled = true;
client_cfg.tls.cert_path = Some(client_cert.path().display().to_string());
client_cfg.tls.key_path = Some(client_key.path().display().to_string());
// client_ca_path intentionally left as None.
```

Then it calls `build_tls_bundle(&client_cfg, Some(ca_cert.path()))`. Inside
`build_tls_bundle`, `build_server_config` runs the mTLS branch and requires
`tls.client_ca_path` (the cert used to verify INCOMING client certs). It is
None → `DistributedTlsError::MissingClientCaPath`.

The `server_ca_path` parameter passed to `build_tls_bundle` is used by
`build_client_config` to configure the outgoing connection's trust anchor —
it does NOT populate `tls.client_ca_path`.

## The tension
- `ft-xbnl0.2.4` tick 337 test `build_tls_bundle_rejects_mtls_without_client_ca_path`
  explicitly pins "Mtls auth without client_ca_path → `MissingClientCaPath`"
  as the intended production contract.
- The failing test expects the opposite (that this config shape succeeds).

## Options
1. Fix the test: set `client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path = Some(ca_cert.path().display().to_string())` alongside the existing cert/key.
2. Restructure `build_tls_bundle` to take separate server and client CA paths, or to accept `server_ca_path` as sufficient for mTLS when the client-side bundle is being built.
3. Deprecate the failing test if the config shape was only ever tentative.

## Attribution
- Authored: jemanuel on 2026-04-10 (commit 147b22176).
- Never fixed or touched since.
- Observed failing on 2026-04-18 during ft-xbnl0.2.4 verification sweep.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The bead-scoped check script (`scripts/check_ft_xbnl0_2_4.sh`) uses `--lib tls_`
filter which does not catch this test (the name has `mtls` with no `tls_`
substring). All 73 tests in that filter pass. The failure surfaces only on
broader `--lib distributed::tests::` filter.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.2`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:36.191788Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047438Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:11:31.047196Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 362 (commit c799aad4): populated client_cfg.tls.client_ca_path in the test, matching the tick-337 positive-guard contract. Broad distributed::tests:: filter now 154/154 ok (was 153/154).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mtls","pre-existing","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-326s9","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.143481Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-3b0gh","title":"[review][LOW] build_trigger_automaton panic message drops the underlying aho-corasick error","description":"commit 4219e912 added crates/frankenterm-core/src/pattern_trigger.rs:272-276:

    Some(
        builder
            .build(patterns)
            .expect("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile"),
    )

The .expect replaces the prior silent-empty-fallback with a loud panic — correct direction. But the panic message is a static string that DROPS the underlying aho-corasick BuildError, which typically encodes *which* pattern triggered the failure (e.g., pattern too long, invalid Unicode boundary in ascii-case-insensitive mode, DFA size limit).

A user hitting this panic in production sees:

    thread 'main' panicked at 'non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile'

…with no indication of *which* rule's pattern broke. Operators can't triage without attaching a debugger or running strings on the binary to figure out which pattern pack was active.

Fix (one-line change):

    .unwrap_or_else(|err| panic!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))

OR use .map_err + .expect:

    .map_err(|err| format!("non-empty trigger pattern sets must compile: {err}"))
    .expect(...)

Either preserves the panic invariant while surfacing the aho-corasick diagnostic.

Minor concern, not a correctness bug. Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23 while verifying ft-lfnwn fix.

Tag: review, pattern-trigger, diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:29.529272Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:29.529272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}

Production behavior should be: delete the dead render_view_placeholder function (lines 1574-1608) since all FTUI views are now implemented and the function is unreachable.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:46.632782Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.111075Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:48:21.110745Z","close_reason":"done — removed dead render_view_placeholder (0 callers, all views migrated) (d83d30d4)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-8qd0l","title":"sync_fts_for_pane skips seq=0 segment on fresh pane (off-by-one in WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq)","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:15884 sync_fts_for_pane`:

```rust
let progress = get_fts_pane_progress_sync(conn, pane_id)?;
let last_seq = progress.as_ref().map_or(0, |p| p.last_indexed_seq);
```

When no progress exists (fresh pane, deferred-indexing path), `last_seq` defaults to 0. Then `get_unindexed_segments_sync` queries with `WHERE seq > ?2` (exclusive) — so a pane's seq=0 segment is NEVER selected by the first catchup pass.

`append_segment_sync` (storage.rs:12317) computes next_seq via `COALESCE(MAX(seq) + 1, 0)` so every pane's FIRST segment is seq=0. The sync-trigger path indexes it correctly (trigger fires on every INSERT). The deferred-catchup path does not.

# Discovery

Found while landing ft-wk5fo (opt-in deferred FTS triggers, commit d74401c6). The initial ft-wk5fo regression tests used seq 0..500 and failed with `99 expected 100` because the first round missed seq=0. Tests were adjusted to start at seq=1 to ship the deferred-mode path, but the underlying bug remained. This bead tracks fixing the bug proper.

# Fix options

(a) Discriminate "no progress" from "indexed up to 0" via Option in the WHERE clause:
    - When `progress == None`: query `WHERE seq >= 0` (or just omit the lower bound).
    - When `progress == Some(p)`: query `WHERE seq > p.last_indexed_seq`.
    Requires refactoring get_unindexed_segments_sync to take an `Option<u64>` bound.

(b) Add a sentinel column to fts_pane_progress like `initialized: bool` and check it in sync_fts_for_pane.

(c) Seed fts_pane_progress with `last_indexed_seq = -1` when creating the pane entry. Requires i64 column or underflow trick.

(a) is cleanest.

# Regression test

Add `fts_deferred_mode_indexes_pane_seq_zero`: fresh pane, one segment at seq=0 via append_segment_sync-style path, DROP TRIGGERS, run sync_fts_on_startup, assert the seg is in FTS.

# Severity

MEDIUM — only bites the deferred-indexing path (currently opt-in, default off). Trigger path is unaffected. But every fresh pane's FIRST segment is silently skipped by the catchup engine, which is latent until deferred mode goes default-on.

# Related

- ft-wk5fo (parent): introduced the deferred path that exposes this bug. Closed with seq-starts-at-1 workaround in the regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:21.050180Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.883029Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:09:27.882777Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8uj10","title":"event identity key should ignore extracted object insertion order","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:19.767134Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.591423Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:58:49.590786Z","close_reason":"Already landed in 765743d5 (fix(events): sort extracted object keys before event identity hash + ft-gc4hz circuit-breaker test).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-8wt4o","title":"[gap] README runtime_compat quarantine claim not matched by frankenterm-core product paths","description":"README.md:41 claims only the remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses `runtime_compat`; grep shows live product modules still depend on `runtime_compat` in crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:37, crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:5918, crates/frankenterm-core/src/event_stream.rs:599, crates/frankenterm-core/src/distributed.rs:1037, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:15,308.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:15.760863Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.893772Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:02:27.754618Z","close_reason":"Fixed in af925d26 — README runtime_compat boundary claim corrected to acknowledge multiple production seams","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
+{"id":"ft-8ybme","title":"[scan_pipeline] process_chunk allows append-after-flush-pending growth","description":"ChunkedPipelineState::process_chunk only exposes should_flush() as an advisory signal. In crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:221-223 and 450-516, callers can keep appending after the active raw-data buffer has already crossed max_buffer_bytes, so a stalled or forgotten flusher grows trigger_data_buffer/uncompressed_buffer without bound.

Repro:
1. let pipeline = ScanPipeline::default();
2. let mut state = ChunkedPipelineState::new(64);
3. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'x'; 80], &mut state);
4. state.should_flush() == true
5. pipeline.process_chunk(&vec![b'y'; 80], &mut state);
6. raw replay buffer keeps growing instead of failing fast or forcing a flush.

Expected: once flush is pending, a subsequent non-empty process_chunk call should refuse to accumulate more bytes rather than silently converting backpressure into unbounded memory growth.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:00.532379Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:55:55.244107Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9unrg","title":"frankenterm-core no-default-features fails: ars_drift tests miss DriftMonitor import","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:09:49.756811Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370803Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:08:46.370517Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-9xbxe","title":"mcp_tools test helper hits asupersync vs fastmcp_rust::asupersync version conflict","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812\` cannot compile under
\`--features mcp\`:

\`\`\`
error[E0308]: mismatched types
    --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:4812:25
     |
4812 |         McpContext::new(asupersync::Cx::for_testing(), 1)
     |         --------------- ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
     |                         expected \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\`,
     |                         found \`asupersync::Cx\`
     |
note: there are multiple different versions of crate \`asupersync\` in
      the dependency graph
\`\`\`

\`McpContext::new\` (defined in \`fastmcp_rust\`) expects
\`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` (i.e. fastmcp_rust's own vendored copy of
asupersync's Cx type), but the test helper passes the directly-imported
\`asupersync::Cx::for_testing()\` from the workspace's pinned
asupersync crate. With version drift between fastmcp_rust's vendored
asupersync (0.2.6 per the older fastmcp_rust pin) and the workspace
asupersync (0.2.9 per /Users/jemanuel/.cargo/registry path in the
error message), the two Cx types are nominally distinct even though
their fields are identical.

## Concrete failure path

1. Run \`cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core --features mcp\`.
2. Compiler tries to build the \`#[cfg(feature = "mcp")]\` test module
   in mcp_tools.rs.
3. \`test_mcp_context()\` at line 4811-4813 fails to type-check because
   \`McpContext::new\` from fastmcp_rust expects \`fastmcp_rust::Cx\` but
   the call site passes \`asupersync::Cx\` from the workspace's
   asupersync.
4. **Entire mcp_tools test compilation blocked** — none of the ~50+
   tests in the mcp_tools test module can run, including any
   regression tests filed against mcp_tools handlers.

## Reproduction

\`\`\`
CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang \\
CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ \\
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rv-p0-target \\
cargo test --no-run --lib -p frankenterm-core \\
  --no-default-features --features mcp 2>&1 | grep -B2 -A10 E0308
\`\`\`

## Severity rationale

- Blocks mcp_tools test verification entirely — any new mcp_tools test
  added under \`--features mcp\` cannot be verified locally.
- Real defect-finding work in the mcp surface (e.g. ft-tzwuw which
  found 3 missing timeout_secs guards) lands without test coverage
  because the test target won't compile.
- Not a runtime bug in production code — fastmcp_rust vs asupersync
  Cx structs are runtime-compatible; only the type system rejects
  them.

## Fix sketch

One of:
1. Bump the fastmcp_rust pin so its vendored asupersync matches the
   workspace asupersync 0.2.9 (preferred — single source of truth).
2. Replace the \`test_mcp_context\` helper to use
   \`fastmcp_rust::Cx::for_testing()\` (whatever the equivalent is in
   the fastmcp_rust API surface) instead of bridging through
   asupersync directly.
3. Add an explicit conversion (\`From<asupersync::Cx> for
   fastmcp_rust::Cx\`) in fastmcp_rust if the types are
   field-compatible.

(1) is the cleanest because it eliminates the duplicated dependency.
(2) is the smallest local change.

## Scope

Diagnostic only — fix likely lives in fastmcp_rust pin (Cargo.toml)
or the test helper. Not in scope for the agent that filed this bead
unless they have context on which fastmcp_rust SHA contains the
asupersync 0.2.9 alignment.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:52:59.938463Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989722Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:12:26.989533Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-a0up5","title":"ingest::trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes returns more than max_bytes when last char straddles the cap","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs:1405\` — \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes(text, max_bytes)\`
silently returns a string LARGER than \`max_bytes\` when the last
character would not fit. The function name and downstream callers
expect a hard \`max_bytes\` cap.

## Concrete failure path

Trace \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 1)\` (中 is 3 UTF-8 bytes):

1. text.len()=3, max_bytes=1. \`text.len() <= max_bytes\` (3 <= 1) is
   false; max_bytes != 0; continue.
2. start = 3.saturating_sub(1) = 2. Position 2 is in the middle of 中.
3. Snap-forward loop: is_char_boundary(2)=false → start=3.
   is_char_boundary(3)=true (text.len() is always a boundary). But the
   loop guard is \`start < text.len()\`, which is false at start=3.
   Exit with start=3.
4. \`start >= text.len()\` (3 >= 3) && max_bytes > 0 → enter the
   "fall back to last full character" branch (line 1419-1430).
5. The fallback walks backward from text.len()=3 to find the start of
   the last character (position 0 for 中), then \`saturating_sub(1)\`
   off-by-ones to position 0. (The off-by-one here is a separate issue
   from the contract violation but produces the same bytes.)
6. Returns \`text[0..]\` = "中" — **3 bytes when max_bytes was 1**.

Symmetric case: \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("a中", 2)\` returns "中"
(3 bytes when 2 was requested).

## Caller impact

\`enforce_segment_size_for_persistence\` (line 1434) wraps this and
captures \`kept_bytes = truncated_content.len()\` in
\`SegmentSizeEnforcement\`. If \`kept_bytes > max_bytes\`, the
\`SegmentSizeEnforcement.kept_bytes\` field reports the violation
correctly — but the actual segment is still oversized, and:

* The truncation gap reason format string says \`"max_bytes={max}"\` —
  misleading because the kept bytes exceed it.
* Downstream consumers that allocate \`max_segment_bytes\`-sized
  buffers, set FTS5 column truncation, or enforce DB-level CHECK
  constraints on segment length get unexpectedly large input.
* Any future caller that does \`assert!(result.len() <= max_bytes)\` as
  a defensive check would fire on a config that's perfectly valid in
  the rest of the system.

## Severity rationale

- Function-contract violation: name says "to_max_bytes", behavior
  doesn't enforce that.
- Reachable when an operator configures \`max_segment_bytes\` smaller
  than the largest UTF-8 char (4 bytes) AND the segment starts with
  multi-byte content. Realistic only for misconfiguration or hostile
  input designed to exercise edge cases — but the bug exists.
- The inline comment at line 1419-1422 justifies "return the last full
  character rather than returning an empty string" — that's a
  *behavior choice*, but it violates the function's declared contract.
  Either the contract should change (function name + docs) or the
  behavior should respect the cap.

## Fix sketch

Two reasonable directions:

(A) **Strict contract** (preferred — matches function name):
  In the fallback branch, if the last character's byte length exceeds
  max_bytes, return \`String::new()\` rather than returning the
  oversize char. Callers that want "best-effort" can opt in via a
  separate API.

(B) **Document the exception**:
  Rename to \`trim_utf8_tail_with_min_one_char\` or update the doc to
  state that the result MAY exceed max_bytes when max_bytes <
  smallest character. Also update \`enforce_segment_size_for_persistence\`
  to assert / handle the over-cap case explicitly.

## Regression tests

Three tests pin the contract:
- \`trim_utf8_tail_returns_at_most_max_bytes_for_multibyte_char\` —
  \`trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes("中", 1)\` returns a string ≤ 1 byte.
- \`trim_utf8_tail_handles_2_byte_cap_with_3_byte_char\` — same for
  \`("a中", 2)\`.
- \`enforce_segment_size_respects_kept_bytes_le_max_bytes\` — the wrapper
  never produces a segment whose content exceeds max_segment_bytes.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/ingest.rs\` only. Existing tests at
lines 6051-6068 don't cover this case (max_bytes=4 with "café" tests
only the well-fitting path).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:25.710169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:57.324478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:57.324241Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 4668d1bf: trim_utf8_tail_to_max_bytes now respects max_bytes as a hard ceiling. Removed the 'fall back to last full character' branch that returned 3-byte 中 when max_bytes=1 or 2. New behavior: when no suffix fits within max_bytes, return empty — downstream enforce_segment_size_for_persistence already wraps the oversize signal in a Gap segment with a truncation_reason, so an empty-content gap is the sound default. 3 regression tests pin: (1) trim('中', 1) and trim('中', 2) return ≤max_bytes; (2) trim('a中', 2) returns ≤2 bytes; (3) enforce_segment_size_for_persistence on '中' with max_segment_bytes=1 produces kept_bytes ≤ 1.  {"id":"ft-tzwuw","title":"mcp_tools ca.search/ca.view/ca.status accept timeout_secs=0, fire immediate-timeout","description":"## Defect

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` cass tools (ca.search line
590, ca.view line 683, ca.status line 763) all pass
\`params.timeout_secs\` directly to \`cass_client_with_timeout()\` without
validating that it's >= 1.

The JSON-Schema declarations for these tools advertise
\`"timeout_secs": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 1, "maximum": 600,
"default": 15 }\` (lines 526, 636, 726), but \`serde_json::from_value\`
does not enforce JSON-Schema bounds — it happily deserializes \`0\` as
a valid u64.

## Concrete failure path

1. MCP client (or any caller that doesn't pre-validate against the
   schema) sends \`{"query": "x", "timeout_secs": 0}\` to ca.search.
2. \`serde_json::from_value\` accepts the 0 → \`params.timeout_secs = 0\`.
3. \`cass_client_with_timeout(0)\` constructs a CassClient with
   \`timeout = Duration::from_secs(0)\` (cass.rs:781-784).
4. \`run_with_cx\` (cass.rs:961) calls
   \`timeout_with_cx(cx, Duration::from_secs(0), cmd.output()).await\`
   — a zero-duration timeout fires immediately, before \`cmd.output()\`
   has any chance to execute.
5. Returns \`CassError::Timeout { timeout_secs: 0 }\` to the MCP client
   on EVERY call. The client sees a confusing "cass timeout (0 secs)"
   error and may not realize the issue is its own \`timeout_secs: 0\`
   parameter rather than a slow cass binary.

Same defect shape as ft-t62hq (already fixed for wa.wait_for and
wa.send tools at lines 1149, 1873) — three sibling cass tools were
missed in that sweep.

## Severity rationale

- Silent UX failure: caller's request always times out without ever
  invoking the underlying tool.
- Reachable via any client that doesn't validate against the
  advertised schema (in practice: most language SDKs that just pass
  through user-supplied JSON, plus any test fixture that omits
  timeout_secs default-checking).
- Not a crash, not data corruption.

## Fix sketch

Mirror the ft-t62hq guard at the top of each \`call(\)\` for the three
cass tools:

\`\`\`rust
if params.timeout_secs == 0 {
    let envelope = McpEnvelope::<()>::error(
        MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS,
        "timeout_secs must be >= 1 (got 0)".to_string(),
        Some(
            "The ca.<tool> schema declares timeout_secs with \\
             minimum: 1; omit the field to use the default (15)."
                .to_string(),
        ),
        elapsed_ms(start),
    );
    return envelope_to_content(envelope);
}
\`\`\`

## Regression tests

Three integration-style tests at the params layer:
- \`ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_view_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`
- \`ca_status_rejects_zero_timeout_secs\`

Each invokes the tool with \`timeout_secs: 0\` and asserts the response
is an Error envelope with \`code = MCP_ERR_INVALID_ARGS\` and the
"timeout_secs must be >= 1" message — proving the guard fires before
\`cass_client_with_timeout\` is ever called.

## Scope

\`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs\` only. No schema changes —
the schemas already declare \`minimum: 1\`. No changes to \`cass.rs\` —
\`with_timeout_secs(0)\` is a documented "instant timeout" primitive
that other callers may legitimately want.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:39:11.699057Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687876Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:15.687498Z","close_reason":"Landed in db5bfeaf (fix(mcp_tools): reject timeout_secs=0 in ca.{search,view,status})","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

E0063 blocks cargo check/build of frankenterm-core lib. Fix: add include_full_cwd: false (or appropriate default) to both constructors, or switch to ..Default::default() base.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:01:43.800293Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:10:02.079758Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:10:02.079437Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-uj5c6","title":"patterns.rs dedup compile break blocks qiba0 MCP golden harness","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T07:04:07.653677Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810694Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:22:43.810041Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5dcea11e","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-uokgq","title":"[review][MEDIUM] release.yml Windows smoke test silently swallows native command failures","description":"commit 2f25ea5d added a Windows smoke test step using `shell: pwsh` at .github/workflows/release.yml:82-96. The script invokes the ft.exe binary via the PowerShell call operator (`& $binary --version`), but does NOT configure PowerShell to fail on native command errors:

    - name: Smoke test binary (Windows)
      if: runner.os == 'Windows'
      shell: pwsh
      run: |
        $binary = "target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/${{ matrix.artifact }}"
        Write-Host "=== ft --version ==="
        & $binary --version
        ...
        & $binary doctor --json
        Write-Host "=== All smoke tests passed ==="

GitHub Actions default pwsh invocation is `pwsh -command ". '{0}'"` — no `-ErrorActionPreference Stop`, no `$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference`. When a native command like `ft doctor --json` exits non-zero:
- PowerShell sets `$LASTEXITCODE` but does NOT throw.
- The script continues past the failing command.
- The last statement is `Write-Host '=== All smoke tests passed ==='` which exits 0.
- The step's final exit code is 0 → GitHub Actions reports success.

Effect: a Windows-only regression where ft.exe panics at startup, crashes on --help, or exits non-zero from doctor would ship to release WITHOUT the workflow catching it. The whole point of the ft-ych74 fix was to close this gap for Windows — and this silently defeats it.

The Unix counterpart at :68-80 (`shell: bash` default) uses GitHub Actions' default args `bash --noprofile --norc -eo pipefail {0}` → `set -e` is implicit, any non-zero exit aborts the step immediately.

Fix (add at top of pwsh script block):

    $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'
    $PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference = $true

`$PSNativeCommandUseErrorActionPreference` (PowerShell 7.3+) makes native command failures throw terminating errors under $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop'. pwsh on ubuntu-latest / windows-latest is 7.4+, so this is safe to enable.

Alternative: check `$LASTEXITCODE` after each `& $binary ...` call and `throw` explicitly.

Severity: MEDIUM. Defeats the intent of 2f25ea5d (which closed my earlier bead ft-ych74). Discovered during reviewer audit round 9, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, release, ci, windows, smoke-test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:20.675627Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:54:20.675627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-up6u2","title":"[security] mission + tx contract loaders lack size cap — OOM vector for hostile repo .ft/mission/*.json","description":"# Bug

`mcp_load_mission_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:257) and
`mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path` (mcp_missions.rs:141) both
use bare `std::fs::read_to_string(path)` with no stat-before-read
size guard.

Pattern-pack loader has this exact guard (ft-05hfm, MAX_PACK_BYTES =
16 MiB). Mission files under `.ft/mission/*.json` have the same
trust property as patterns under `.ft/patterns/*.{yaml,json,toml}` —
a hostile repo can ship them, and they're loaded at MCP tool
invocation time.

# Attack

1. Hostile repo ships `.ft/mission/active.json` = 5 GiB of garbage.
2. Operator (or agent via wa.mission_state MCP) opens the project.
3. `wa.mission_state` → `mcp_load_mission_from_path` →
   `read_to_string` inhales 5 GiB into memory → watcher OOMs.

Same attack for `.ft/mission/tx-active.json` via wa.tx_show / wa.tx_run.

`resolve_workspace_scoped_path` (mcp_missions.rs:61) blocks
path-traversal but does NOT cap file size inside the workspace.

# Fix

Add `MAX_MISSION_BYTES` + `MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES` constants (16 MiB
each, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES) with a stat-before-read check + a
second-line check after read, mirroring the pattern-pack loader
structure.

Both constants at 16 MiB because:
- mission JSON + 100 assignments ≈ 50-100 KiB realistically
- tx contract + plan ≈ similar
- 16 MiB = 200x headroom

# Tests

Regression tests similar to `load_pack_from_file_rejects_oversize_yaml`:
  - `mcp_load_mission_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`
  - `mcp_load_mission_tx_contract_from_path_rejects_oversize_json`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:25:19.149935Z","created_by":"pane3-mission-cap","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408462Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:21.408112Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3e2ae80c. Added MAX_MISSION_BYTES and MAX_TX_CONTRACT_BYTES (both 16 MiB, matching MAX_PACK_BYTES from ft-05hfm) + shared read_capped_file helper + 2 regression tests (load_tx_contract_rejects_oversize_json, load_mission_rejects_oversize_json). Preserved historical robot.tx_not_found / robot.mission_not_found error codes on NotFound branch. Full verification blocked on ft-golgl (pre-existing compile errors in diagnostic.rs, outcome.rs, workflows/handlers.rs under --features mcp); changes reviewed against the ft-05hfm pattern shipping on HEAD.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","mission","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-uqlrq","title":"[mock] search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36 embed() is a stub that always errors","description":"search/model2vec_embedder.rs:36-38 has `fn embed(&self, _text: &str) -> Result<Vec<f32>, EmbedError> { // Stub: real implementation loads ONNX model; Err(EmbedError::ModelNotFound(...)) }`. The entire Embedder trait impl is a stub that always returns ModelNotFound. Zero production callers exist — tests in search/mod.rs and daemon/worker.rs explicitly verify model2vec is "retired" and rejected. Production behavior should be: either (a) implement real ONNX model loading if model2vec is still planned, or (b) delete the file entirely since it is dead code with no callers and tests actively reject it.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:32:43.678300Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389463Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:52:17.389208Z","close_reason":"Shipped in af925d26. Audit confirmed the file was orphaned:
- not declared in search/mod.rs (no 'mod model2vec_embedder;' anywhere)
- zero constructors of Model2VecEmbedder outside the file itself
- three tests across the tree explicitly verify model2vec is RETIRED (never advertised as a tier, rejected as a selector, rejected by the embed worker): search::tests::advertised_embedder_tiers_never_reports_retired_model2vec_backend, search::daemon::worker::tests::build_embedder_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector, tests/semantic_backend_selection_integration.rs::embed_worker_rejects_retired_model2vec_selector

Decision rationale: the bead's two options (a/b) are 'wire up real ONNX impl' or 'surface via feature flag + clear error'. Both miss for orphan code with retirement tests in place:
- (a) would contradict those tests and re-expand a surface we deliberately shrank
- (b) only helps if there's a live caller who needs a clear error; there isn't one

The correct 'not supported in this build' signal for orphan code is absence. Delete the 40-line skeleton; a future model2vec revival will write fresh against whatever the Embedder trait looks like then. Also updated the stale 'e.g. Model2Vec distilled' comment on ManagedEmbedderStack.fast in orchestrator.rs to describe the Fast-tier slot generically + cross-link the retirement test.

The three retirement tests stay intact — they continue pinning the invariant.

Verification (rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target):
- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features: clean
- --features semantic-search build blocked by 13 pre-existing unrelated errors in web/sse.rs (select! macro fallout from the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip); my patch only removes a file + edits a doc comment so it cannot introduce compile errors

Note: the commit that carries the change is misleadingly titled 'docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim' — another agent's linter bundled my uncommitted model2vec deletion + orchestrator comment into its own commit. The 44 lines of change are correct (40 deletions in model2vec_embedder.rs + 4 lines in orchestrator.rs doc comment); only the commit title under-describes the scope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-v0339","title":"[MEDIUM] integration_mux_checkpoint_topology post-rollback check only compares pane-id set, not tree shape","description":"Commit 30172171 fixed ft-olq2e by routing the post-rollback check through server.registry().snapshot() instead of the vacuous re-deserialization of initial_topo_json. However the new primary assertion at integration_mux_checkpoint_topology.rs still only compares PANE IDs:

  post_rollback_pane_ids_sorted == baseline_pane_ids

TopologySnapshot (crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_topology.rs) is a tree of windows → tabs → panes, with per-window position/size/title and per-tab pane layout metadata. The registry snapshot, filtered to LifecycleEntityKind::Pane, flattens all of that to a bag of pane u64 ids.

Consequence: a RemoteRequest::Rollback that restores the correct set of pane entities but (a) reparents them under the wrong tab, (b) drops a window or tab node, (c) corrupts the split tree under a tab, or (d) restores panes in the wrong order will still pass the assertion. Rollback tree-shape correctness is therefore still not covered by this test — the bead it closes was motivated precisely by false confidence in rollback behavior.

Expected fix:
- Reconstruct a post-rollback TopologySnapshot from the live server (registry + whatever stores tab/window edges) and compare it field-by-field against the pre-expansion baseline — at least window ids + tab ids + pane ids grouped by tab.
- Keep the pane-id-set assertion as a weaker redundant guard.

Regression risk: medium. The test name promises topology validation; reviewers relying on this bead's closure will assume tree-structure regressions are caught when in fact only pane existence is.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:02:21.495704Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017561Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:19:22.017321Z","close_reason":"Strengthened rollback validation to require exact TopologySnapshot restoration, not just pane-id/fingerprint agreement","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mux","review","session-persistence","test-gap"]}
